text:
In the verdant heart of the Whispering Woods, under skies so vividly blue they seemed to sing, there lived a squirrel named Rufus. Rufus loved nothing more than exploring the intricate web of oak branches that swayed gently in the breezes. One bright morning, despite a looming storm, he decided to visit Mrs. Oakleaves.

Mrs. Oakleaves lived high up in a magnificent specimen of an oak tree. Rufus knew the journey well—he had traveled it dozens of times. Just as he reached the gnarled branch where she lived, down swooped a shiny new hawk. It eyed Rufus with a predator's curiosity before shooting down into the woods with great speed.

Rufus paused only a moment. Instead of chasing after the hawk, he picked up one of his acorn caches and scurried away. He found a secure hollow under a great obsidian stone and clumped himself into a comfortable nest of leaves.
